title: [Feature]: Evaluate a generic JMS sink
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material includes Java Message Service and names messaging systems such as IBM MQ and RabbitMQ in the JMS family. nats-sinks does not yet have a generic JMS sink.
proposal: Research a generic JMS sink that can publish normalized NatsEnvelope records to JMS queues or topics through a safe Python-to-JMS integration approach. The evaluation should define how provider-specific clients, JNDI, persistent delivery, message properties, transactions, duplicate handling, and payload encoding are handled without turning the core package into a Java runtime.
users: Enterprise integration teams with existing JMS, IBM MQ, RabbitMQ, or Java middleware estates.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires persistent delivery and a transaction or confirmed-send boundary for every required message. Non-persistent delivery must be disabled by default or marked unsafe for durable event custody.
security: Credentials, broker URLs, queue names, headers, and payloads must be redacted. TLS verification, authentication, and least-privilege queue permissions must be documented.
acceptance: Research selects one or more viable integration approaches.
The design separates generic JMS behavior from Oracle WebLogic-specific behavior.
Persistent delivery and transaction requirements are documented.
Unit tests use fake clients and no external brokers.
Documentation covers provider compatibility and limitations.
tests: Add contract tests with fake JMS clients for config validation, message property mapping, persistent send confirmation, duplicate handling, and error translation.
documentation: Add generic JMS sink research documentation and update sink roadmap.
closeout: Close after the research or implementation release is published.
